<!-- Generated from sibling .json by scripts/spec-trace/Render-SpecTraceMarkdownFromJson.ps1. Do not edit this file directly. -->  # ARC-QUIC-CRT-0009 - QUIC Client Certificate Acceptance Policy Architecture  ## Purpose  Describe the permanent client-side local certificate-acceptance policy seam that lives above the managed TLS proof states, consumes a pinned leaf SHA-256 fingerprint, surfaces PeerCertificatePolicyAccepted, and keeps peer transport-parameter commit separate from cryptographic proof without claiming trust, hostname validation, or full handshake support.  ## Requirements Satisfied  - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0103 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0109 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0111  ## Design Summary  The connection runtime keeps transcript progression, managed handshake proof, and local certificate acceptance as separate but coordinated owners. The existing transcript-progress owner remains responsible for ordered Handshake CRYPTO fragments, handshake message parsing, and staged transport-parameter metadata. The managed key-schedule owner derives handshake traffic secrets, handshake packet-protection material, and proof states for the explicit client-only subset, including PeerCertificateVerifyVerified and PeerFinishedVerified. Above those proof states, a new permanent local acceptance seam compares a pinned leaf SHA-256 fingerprint against the leaf certificate facts surfaced by the managed proof path. The bridge publishes PeerCertificatePolicyAccepted only when the proof state exists and the fingerprint matches, leaves no-policy configurations blocked without silent acceptance, and routes mismatches through the existing fatal/update path. PeerHandshakeTranscriptCompleted remains a transcript milestone rather than a policy claim, and peer transport-parameter commit remains unavailable until proof and policy acceptance are both present. Server-role commit remains unavailable until equivalent cryptographic coverage exists.  ## Key Components  - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTranscriptProgress.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicHandshakeFlowCoordinator.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsKeySchedule.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsCipherSuiteProfile.cs  ## Risks  - The key-schedule owner must stay permanently narrow and client-only; it must not grow into a general TLS subsystem. - The supported subset must stay pinned to TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1 until a later requirement explicitly widens it. - PeerCertificateVerifyVerified must not be mistaken for certificate trust, certificate-path validation, hostname validation, or identity authentication. - PeerCertificatePolicyAccepted must not be mistaken for certificate trust, certificate-path validation, hostname validation, revocation, or identity authentication. - PeerFinishedVerified must not be mistaken for certificate trust, identity authentication, or CertificateVerify signature verification. - The local acceptance seam must stay pinned to a narrow, permanent policy shape and must not silently accept when unconfigured. - Unsupported or malformed cipher-suite or key-share input must fail deterministically rather than silently falling back.  ## Related Code And Tests  - [`src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs`](../../../src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs) -
